SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF PETITIONERS CALPERS' MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. THE DOCTRINE OF EXCLUSIVE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION NOT APPLICABLE. THE SACRAMENTO COUNTY AND ALAMEDA COUNTY CASES INVOLVED DIFFERENT PARTIES, AND DIFFERENT ALLEGATIONS. FURTHER, DEFENDANT DID NOT RAISE THE ISSUE BY FILING A MOTION FOR STAY OR A DEMURRER/PLEA IN ABATEMENT. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 19851 AND 19849 GIVE THE EXECUTIVE BROAD AUTHORITY TO CONTROL THE WORK WEEK. NO SHOWING THAT THE GOVERNOR'S EXECUTIVE ORDERS WERE ULTRA VIRES. THE GOVERNOR ACTED REASONABLY IN FURLOUGHING ALL EMPLOYEES TO SAVE FUNDS AND PRESERVE PARITY. THE DPA'S DECISION NOT TO EXEMPT PETITIONER WAS ALSO REASONABLE TO PRESERVE FUNDS AND MAINTAIN PARITY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR #10276
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-18-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE: CALPERS' MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE (DENIED)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Moiton For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM Motion for Peremptory Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Jeffrey Ryan Rieger, Esq. & Harvey L. Leiderman, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (510) 273-8832 appearing for Petitioner CalPersBoard and moving party; Will M Yamada, Esq. Dept. of Personnel Adm., State of California, (916) 324-0512 appearing for Resp. State of California and opposing party; David W. Tyra, Esq. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedemann & Girard (916) 321-4500 also appearing for Respondents; Ross C. Moody, Esq. Dept. of Justice, State of California, appearing for State Controller John Ching. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction not applicable. The Sacramento County and Alameda County cases involved different parties, and different allegations. Further, defendant did not raise the issue by filing a motion for stay or a demurrer/plea in abatement. Government Code sections 19851 and 19849 give the Executive broad authority to control the work week. No showing that the Governor's executive orders were ultra vires. The Governor acted reasonably in furloughing all employees to save funds and preserve parity. The DPA's decision not to exempt petitioner was also reasonable to preserve funds and maintain parity. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan #10276
